topic: EXP-434 integration — snapshot-date anchoring of p4-424 opening paragraph
change_summary: Decider commit 2f890a1 (2026-05-27T02:43Z) replaced the one-sentence opening paragraph of SEC-4.2.4 (`<details id="p4-424">`) with a three-sentence paragraph anchoring the four E-PRED sub-test enumeration to the 2026-03-12 raw-text/03-predictions.txt snapshot and recording the 2026-05-07 live-page state (only E-PRED-B explicitly labeled; A/C/D folded into Tier 1/2/3 protocol). Patch source: EXP-432-baby-batch-2.json (the EXP-432→EXP-434 rename in commit e9f33bb was an ID-collision fix; batch files retained original prefix). ISS-1877 closed in same commit.
our_argument_summary: SEC-4.2.4 examines the four E-PRED sub-tests (A peak-tracks-geometry, B Hartland-beats-Ebro, C SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal, D non-path stations below noise floor) registered for the August 2026 eclipse and finds three non-discriminating + one (E-PRED-C, gravity null) that actually favors the standard ionospheric model over the dome's instantaneous-coupling claim. The new opening paragraph anchors the enumeration to the 2026-03-12 dome snapshot and flags the post-snapshot live-page restructuring as not retiring the prediction commitments.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level review — N/A at WIN granularity. The E-PRED-B prediction in monitor.py uses hardcoded ecm_eclipse = -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 literals (see SEC-4.2.4 body) for the dome's on-disk monitor; the live dome page does fetch eclipse-day station data when available, but the prediction commitments themselves are the static 2026-03-12 registry plus the post-snapshot Tier 1/2/3 protocol.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: E-PRED-A (peak tracks geometry) and E-PRED-B (Hartland>Ebro) both reduce to standard Chapman-Sq mechanism predictions on a spherical Earth. E-PRED-D (non-path stations below noise floor) is also predicted by standard physics. Only E-PRED-C makes a discriminating prediction — and the body shows it favors the standard model.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The four E-PRED sub-tests were registered 2026-03-12 (raw-text/03-predictions.txt EXTRACTED date matches) for an August 2026 eclipse that has not yet occurred. The predictions are prospective in scope — the issue our review flags is post-snapshot restructuring (Tier 1/2/3 protocol replacing the labeled E-PRED enumeration), not retrofitting after results.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Per the E-PRED-B body analysis, FSF = H(r)/r reduces to tan(latitude)/tan(ref_lat) by the dome's own placement equation r×tan(lat)=H(r) — any firmament height function cancels algebraically. The numerical predictions are not derived from dome geometry; they are tan(latitude) restated.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Section is descriptive prose plus per-sub-test analysis — no calculation pipeline. The body cites monitor.py line 512 (hardcoded ecm_eclipse literal) and fsf_derivation.py (curve-fitting confirmation) but the new opening paragraph itself does no computation.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: New opening paragraph: descriptive. Body per-sub-test paragraphs: extensively document hardcoded literals.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None in the new paragraph. The original prose carried the rhetorical "intellectually dishonest" framing; new paragraph preserves it with the qualifier "quietly relabeled," which is the substantive claim a defender must rebut.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. The integrated paragraph matches EXP-432-baby-batch-2.replace_pattern verbatim (curly-vs-straight-quote normalization aside). The raw-text/03-predictions.txt link target is git-tracked and contains the four E-PRED labels exactly as enumerated in the paragraph. EXTRACTED date 2026-03-12 in the raw-text header matches the snapshot-date anchor in the paragraph. EXP-292.verification_performed confirms the 2026-05-07 live-page state (E-PRED-B only explicitly labeled; A/C/D absent).

holes_found.description: Cross-page inconsistency vs SEC-8b.3 L3a. The SEC-8b.3 parallel paragraph (patched by EXP-292, explicitly named as the analogy_to for EXP-432-baby-batch-2) includes the explicit E-PRED-C inversion sentence: "Most consequentially, E-PRED-C inverted between snapshots — the 2026-03-12 page predicted SG gravimeters would read 0.0 µGal during the eclipse; the 2026-05-07 live page replaces it with PRED-TIER-3's positive 10–13 µGal pulse for the same instrument class at the same August 2026 eclipse (see WIN-014 for the resulting self-contradiction analysis)." The SEC-4.2.4 opening paragraph integrated by EXP-432-baby-batch-2 omits this sentence and relies solely on "folded without WITHDRAWN notation into the dome's restructured Tier 1/2/3 κ-coupling protocol" — which understates the case for E-PRED-C. I grep-confirmed that none of "Tier 3", "10-13", "10–13", "positive pulse", "inverted", or "reversed" appears anywhere in the SEC-4.2.4 section (the per-sub-test E-PRED-C paragraph below also does not call out the inversion — it focuses on the WIN-012 κ contradiction). A dome defender can correctly counter: "You are holding us to an OLD commitment we have explicitly superseded. The new Tier 3 prediction (+10–13 µGal pulse) is the OPPOSITE of E-PRED-C; that is replacement, not a fold, and your charge of 'quiet relabeling' is uncharitable." SEC-4.2.4 is the main eclipse discussion (more discoverable than SEC-8b.3, which sits in the response-to-critics tab); readers landing on SEC-4.2.4 first get the weaker framing and miss the strongest evidence that the dome's OWN active protocol now contradicts the registered E-PRED-C prediction across versions.
holes_found.recommendation: Mirror the SEC-8b.3 E-PRED-C inversion sentence into either (a) the SEC-4.2.4 opening paragraph, immediately after "folded without WITHDRAWN notation into the dome's restructured Tier 1/2/3 κ-coupling protocol;" or (b) the E-PRED-C per-sub-test paragraph. Option (a) keeps cross-page parity; option (b) lets the per-sub-test discussion deepen the κ-contradiction argument with the inversion fact. WIN-014's detail_evidence already discusses the 10–13 µGal target via the κ = 1.677 nT/µGal cluster — cite it explicitly. EXP-432-baby-batch-2 self-described its scope as "per-sub-test analysis in body is unchanged and remains correct" with the snapshot-date framing localized to the opening paragraph; the inversion call-out is a separate, additive sentence that belongs in either surface.

holes_found.description: TLDR present-tense framing not updated alongside body. The SEC-4.2.4 TLDR still reads: "The dome registers four 'model-discriminating' sub-tests (E-PRED-A through D)." After the body patch, the body says "registered four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests" anchored to the 2026-03-12 snapshot. A skim-reader who sees only the TLDR gets present-tense framing that conflicts with the body's acknowledgement that the live page no longer explicitly labels three of the four. The scare quotes around "model-discriminating" partially carry the dome's framing, which is why this is minor rather than moderate. EXP-432-baby-batch-2.integration_notes explicitly noted "TLDR already corrected (E-PRED-C, gravity null) per ISS-1877 partial fix 2026-05-08" — that fix addressed the E-PRED-C content but not the present-tense framing.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional minor edit: change "The dome registers four" to "The dome's 2026-03-12 snapshot registered four" or similar to carry the snapshot-date provenance into the TLDR. Low priority — the scare quotes already do most of the work, and TLDR length budget matters.

stronger_arguments.description: Add an explicit WIN-014 cross-reference to the SEC-4.2.4 E-PRED-C per-sub-test paragraph (mirror what SEC-8b.3 L3a does). WIN-014's detail_evidence already discusses the κ-cluster vise and the 10–13 µGal target as "the only published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target (which, through κ = 1.677 nT/µGal, back-implies an unstated August 2026 ΔB premise of 16.8–21.8 nT at the named target stations — at or above the upper edge of the global eclipse-magnetic typical totality ensemble of 5–15 nT cited for WIN-068)." That argument deepens the E-PRED-C analysis substantially and is currently confined to WIN-014.
sources: data/sections.json part8b SEC-8b.3 L3a paragraph (cross-page parity model)

stronger_arguments.description: Anchor the "intellectually dishonest" rhetorical claim more tightly. The dome's OWN public framing (raw-text/03-predictions.txt header: "PROSPECTIVE: Timestamped before confirming data (Highest evidential weight — true advance prediction)") is the load-bearing fact — silent relabeling without WITHDRAWN tags is not merely sloppy; it directly undermines the pre-registration claim that the dome itself foregrounds as "highest evidential weight." A one-clause addition citing the dome's own "PROSPECTIVE / Highest evidential weight" language would convert "intellectually dishonest" from a rhetorical assertion into a defender-quoted self-contradiction.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome team did publicly update the eclipse-prediction protocol with a new Tier 1/2/3 structure that carries timestamps (per EXP-292.verification_performed snapshot of the 2026-05-07 live page). They did not delete the predictions page or hide the restructuring; a reader visiting the live dome page sees the new Tier protocol with PRED-TIER-3 explicitly described, and the dome's git history preserves the change. A charitable reading is "model iteration as new data informed the prediction structure" rather than "quiet relabeling to dodge falsification" (EXP-432-baby-batch-2.tldr_evidence and the new SEC-4.2.4 paragraph at sections.json:p4-424 acknowledge the restructuring exists as a public artifact).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Model iteration without explicit WITHDRAWN notation is incompatible with the dome's OWN epistemic framing. The 2026-03-12 snapshot at raw-text/03-predictions.txt foregrounds "PROSPECTIVE: Timestamped before confirming data (Highest evidential weight — true advance prediction)" — the entire claim to higher evidential weight rests on the predictions being immutable once timestamped. Silently restructuring (and, for E-PRED-C specifically, INVERTING) those predictions undermines the very pre-registration claim that distinguishes them from postdictions. So holding the dome to the 2026-03-12 enumeration is fair on the dome's own terms, not merely on ours. The new SEC-4.2.4 paragraph (sections.json:p4-424) correctly identifies the post-snapshot restructuring as "quietly relabeled" — the kernel-of-truth charitable reading would only save the claim if the dome had not built its credibility brand on cryptographic timestamping in the first place.

deeper_analysis: The patch was applied cleanly (verbatim match to EXP-432-baby-batch-2.replace_pattern modulo curly-quote conversion in sections.json), the raw-text link target exists and is git-tracked, the snapshot date 2026-03-12 matches raw-text/03-predictions.txt EXTRACTED header, and the four E-PRED labels exactly match raw-text content. 3692/3692 tests pass after integration; docs/index.html was rebuilt and contains the new paragraph twice (once in SEC-4.2.4, once in SEC-8b.3). The verdict on E-PRED-C as the discriminating sub-test that actually favors the standard model holds; the verdict on E-PRED-A, B, D as non-discriminating holds. The integration is structurally sound. The single substantive finding is the cross-page inconsistency with SEC-8b.3: that section's parallel paragraph explicitly calls out the E-PRED-C inversion (0.0 µGal → +10–13 µGal pulse) while SEC-4.2.4 does not, leaving the main eclipse discussion with the weaker framing. EXP-432-baby-batch-2's analogy_to field explicitly names EXP-292 (the SEC-8b.3 patch) as the model — but the inversion sentence was either deliberately or accidentally omitted from the SEC-4.2.4 version. The strongest argument we have (the dome's own active protocol now contradicts the registered E-PRED-C prediction) is currently behind the response-to-critics tab rather than in the main eclipse analysis. Recommended: minor_edit to add the inversion sentence to SEC-4.2.4.

Integration commit 2f890a1 (2026-05-27T02:43Z) closed ISS-1877, integrated EXP-432-baby-batch-2 patch
citations_unchecked: raw-text/03-predictions.txt content reflects the actual dome page state on 2026-03-12 (assumed — would require comparison to git-archived dome HTML if any exists; the raw-text/ file is the operational record)
2026-05-07 live-page state from EXP-292 was not re-fetched in this review; relying on EXP-292.verification_performed (~20 days old)

summary_for_decider: PATCH INTEGRATION CLEAN. One moderate finding: SEC-4.2.4 opening paragraph (EXP-432-baby-batch-2) omits the explicit E-PRED-C inversion sentence (0.0 µGal → +10–13 µGal pulse) that the parallel SEC-8b.3 L3a paragraph (EXP-292) carries. Recommend: minor_edit to mirror the inversion sentence into SEC-4.2.4 opening paragraph OR the E-PRED-C per-sub-test paragraph, with WIN-014 cross-reference. Also a minor TLDR present-tense framing note (low priority — scare quotes carry the dome-framing). No critical/major holes. 3692/3692 tests pass. ISS-1877 closure is correct on the snapshot-date-anchoring scope; the cross-page inversion-call-out is a NEW finding, not a re-opening of ISS-1877.
